content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • •• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance Administration         Executive Legal         Enforcement         Conflict of Interest
916 322 5660                                322 5901             322 6441             327 6444


October 7, 1977


Edythe Campbell
City Clerk
City of Berkeley
2134 Grove Street
Berkeley, California 94704

Dear Ms. Campbell:

Thank you for your letter of August 4, 1977, seeking advice
with respect to the cash contributions of the City
Council of Berkeley. Please excuse this tardy response.

With respect to your first question, the City of Berkeley
Board of Education is not a city agency. The appropriate code
reviewing body for the Board of Education of the City of Berkeley
is the Board of Supervisors in which the school district is
located. See, Government Code Section 3201l(b).

Next, with respect to the Conflict of Interest Code of the
Berkeley Redevelopment Agency, please be advised that as a city
agency, the Redevelopment Agency must submit a Conflict of Interest
Code to the City Council covering its designated employees.
However, since pursuant to City Resolution 48,270, Section 2,
the Conflict of Interest Code of the members of the City Council
of Berkeley is applicable to City Council members as "members
of the City Council or as members of any City Board or Commission",
if the City Council reconstitutes itself as the Redevelopment
Agency, there is no need to make City Council members designated
employees within the Code of the Redevelopment Agency.

Second, you ask whether the City Manager should be included
within the City Council Code or in any Code. The City Manager
should be included in a Code to be submitted to the City Council
by the Office of the City Manager. Because the City Manager
is required to disclose pursuant to Government Code Section
37200, no additional disclosure responsibilities can be imposed

Edythe Campbell
October 7, 1977
Page Two

upon the City Manager under a Code, however, the City Manager
must be included in a Code for purposes of disqualification.
Thus, the Code for the City Manager would result in all sub-
stantive respects the Code for the City Council.

I trust that the foregoing responds to your questions.
If you have further questions, please feel free to call me at
(916)322-6444.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr.
Chief, Conflicts of Interest
Division

LSD:mfa

CITY OF BERKELEY

CITY CLERK DEPARTMENT                                    (415) 644-6480
2134 GROVE STREET         BERKELEY, CALIFORNIA         94704


August 9, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
926 "J" Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, CA  95814

Gentlemen:

Subject:  Conflict of Interest Codes

I would appreciate your advice on two points:

     1.  In March of 1976, I requested an opinion from FPPC as to
          whether the City of Berkeley Board of Education and the
          Redevelopment Agency should develop conflict of interest
          codes for submittal to the City Council as the code
          reviewing body.

          Nancy Salsman replied on March 10, 1976, that the City
          Council would be the code reviewing body for any city
          agency and that, indeed, they should develop a code for
          review by the Berkeley City Council in their position
          of Code Reviewing Body.

          I would appreciate confirmation that this decision is
          accurate, and that no later decision by FPPC would affect
          our situation.

     2.  The City Council Conflict of Interest Code does not include
          the City Manager, although that position is specifically
          mentioned in Section 87200.  Should (or must) the City Manager
          be included in the City Council Code, should he be specifically
          included in a Code for the City Manager Department, or does
          the fact the position is named in the Government Code mean
          a city manager need not be specifically named in any local
          code?

Your early reply would be appreciated as Berkeley is formulating the process
and timetable for adoption of city-wide codes.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Edythe Campbell
City Clerk

EC:lsp
cc:  Ted Lakey

The appropriate code reviewing body for the Board of Education of the City of Berkeley is the Board of Supervisors in which the school district is located. See, Government Code Section 82011(b). Next, with respect to the Conflict of Interest Code of the Berkeley Redevelopment Agency, please be advised that as a city agency, the Redevelopment Agency must submit a Conflict of Interest Code to the City Council covering its designated employees. However, since pursuant to City Resolution 48,270, Section 2, the Conflict of Interest Code of the members of the City Council of Berkeley is applicable to City Council members as "members of the City Council or as members of any City Board or Commission", if the City Council reconstitutes itself as the Redevelopment Agency, there is no need to make City Council members designated employees within the Code of the Redevelopment Agency. Second, you ask whether the City Manager should be included within the City Council Code or in any Code. The City Manager should be included in a Code to be submitted to the City Council by the Office of the City Manager. Because the City Manager is required to disclose pursuant to Government Code Section 87200, no additional disclosure responsibilities can be imposed. Thus, the Code for the City Manager would resemble in all substantive respects the Code for the City Council. I trust that the foregoing responds to your questions. If you have further questions, please feel free to call me at (916) 322-6444. Sincerely, [signature] Delbert L.
